name: Replica Set Initialization and Management
description: Deploy a MongoDB replica set for high availability with automatic failover, data redundancy, and read scaling across multiple nodes.
prerequisites: MongoDB 6+ installed on 3 servers (or 3 local instances on different ports)

steps.title: Start MongoDB instances with replication enabled
steps.description: Launch three mongod instances with the same replica set name.
steps.tip: Repeat for ports 27018 and 27019 with different dbpath and logpath values.

steps.title: Initialize the replica set
steps.description: Connect to one instance and initialize the replica set with all members.

steps.title: Check replica set status
steps.description: View the current state of all replica set members and identify the primary.

steps.title: Verify replication is working
steps.description: Write data to the primary and read it from a secondary to confirm replication.
steps.command: mongosh --port 27017 --eval 'db.test.insertOne({msg: "replication test", ts: new Date()})' mydb && mongosh --port 27018 --eval 'db.getMongo().setReadPref("secondary"); db.test.find().sort({ts: -1}).limit(1)' mydb

steps.title: View replication lag
steps.description: Check how far behind each secondary is from the primary.

faq.question: What happens when the primary goes down?
faq.answer: The remaining members hold an election and a secondary with the most up-to-date data is promoted to primary. This typically completes in 10-12 seconds. Applications using the replica set connection string reconnect automatically.

faq.question: Why do I need an odd number of members?
faq.answer: Elections require a majority vote. With 3 members, 2 form a majority even if 1 is down. With 2 members, losing one means no majority and no election can happen. Use an arbiter if you can only afford 2 data-bearing nodes.

faq.question: How do I add a new member to an existing replica set?
faq.answer: Start a new mongod with --replSet and the same replica set name. Then on the primary run rs.add('new-host:27017'). MongoDB automatically syncs data to the new member via initial sync.
